name: mapper_node_stability_v1
motivation: Test whether Mapper contributes stable, interpretable local regimes beyond dimensionality reduction and simple controls.

target_object.view_for_matching: raw token source indices plus norm-density Mapper lens
target_object.unit_of_analysis: Mapper node tracks across overlapping bootstrap samples

matching.method: Hungarian one-to-one matching by exact source-token Jaccard

interpretability.visualization: Beans-only patch atlas of all tracks that pass the declared thresholds

controls.bootstrap_unit: overlapping fixed-pool token subsamples; image-block bootstrap is a future stricter guardrail

primary_question: Do observed FLUX token clouds contain more stable label-enriched Mapper node tracks than the strongest matched control?

pass: mean stable_enriched_track_count_delta across datasets is positive
bootstrap 95 percent confidence interval lower bound is above zero
at least two of three datasets have positive delta
decision_rule.fail_interpretation: A failure is evidence that the current Mapper nodes are mainly lens, cover, density, or dataset-size artifacts rather than robust representation regimes.

secondary_readouts: stable track size coefficient of variation
